    "UsedTo": [
      "It is used to cut the scheduled 2,211-mile trip between Chicago and Seattle from 58.5 hours to 45 hours.",
      "It is used to offer passengers sweeping views of the route.",
      "It is used to improve its farebox recovery ratio.",
      "It is used to run through Milwaukee via the Milwaukee Road.",
      "It is used to operate west from the Twin Cities before turning northwest in Willmar.",
      "It is used to reach Fargo.",
      "It is used to reroute the Builder over the route between Fargo and Minot via Grand Forks.",
      "It is used to complement the Builder and North Coast Hiawatha on the segment between Spokane and Seattle.",
      "It is used to provide a three-times-daily service on a long-distance route outside the Silver corridor.",
      "It is used to run combined with the Builder three days a week.",
      "It is used to continue to serve St. Cloud, Staples and Detroit Lakes.",
      "It is used to split in Spokane.",
      "It is used to restore service to the line previously operated by the Spokane, Portland and Seattle Railway.",
      "It is used to split in Spokane for much of the 1940s and 1950s.",
      "It is used to include a wine and cheese tasting in the dining car for sleeping car passengers.",
      "It is used to provide free newspapers in the morning.",
      "It is used to give commentary on points of visual and historic interest.",
      "It is used to return to its pre-pandemic daily schedule."

      "それはシカゴとシアトルまたはポートランド間の乗客輸送に使用されます。",

    "UsedTo": [
      "それはシカゴとシアトル間の2,211マイルの旅を58.5時間から45時間に短縮するために使用されました。",
      "それは乗客にルートの広大な景色を提供するために使用されました。",
      "それは運賃回収率を改善するために使用されました。",
      "それはミルウォーキー・ロード経由でミルウォーキーを通過するために使用されました。",
      "それはツインシティーズからウィルマーを経由して北西に向かう前に、西に向かって運行するために使用されました。",
      "それはファーゴに到達するために使用されました。",
      "それはグランドフォークス経由でファーゴとミノット間のルートを迂回するために使用されました。",
      "それはスポケーンとシアトル間の区間で、ビルダーとノースコースト・ハイアワサを補完するために使用されました。",
      "それはシルバーコリドー外の長距離路線で1日3便の運行を提供するために使用されました。",
      "それは週3日、ビルダーと組み合わせて運行するために使用されました。",
      "それはノースコースト・ハイアワサのキャンセルによりサービスが失われる可能性があったセントクラウド、ステープルズ、デトロイトレイクスを引き続き提供するために使用されました。",
      "それはスポケーンで分割するために使用されました。",
      "それは以前スポケーン・ポートランド・アンド・シアトル鉄道によって運行されていた路線へのサービスを回復するために使用されました。",
      "それは1940年代と1950年代の多くでスポケーンで分割するために使用されました。",
      "それは寝台車乗客のために食堂車でのワインとチーズの試飲を含めるために使用されました。",
      "それは朝の無料新聞を提供するために使用されました。",
      "それは視覚的および歴史的な興味のある地点について解説するために使用されました。",
      "それはパンデミック前の日常スケジュールに戻るために使用されました。"

About: It is about a daily long-distance passenger train operated by Amtrak.
Affects: It affects the Cascade Mountains and Glacier National Park.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with James J. Hill.
HasA: It has two sections west of Spokane.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a travel time of 45–46 hours.
It is characterized by an average speed of about 50 mph.
It is characterized by speeds as fast as 79 mph.
It is characterized by dome coaches.
It is characterized by a full-length Great Dome car for first class passengers.
It is characterized by Superliner I cars.
IsA: It is a daily long-distance passenger train.
It is the flagship passenger train of the Great Northern Railway.
It is Amtrak's busiest long-distance route.
It is Amtrak's most popular long-distance train.
KnownAs: It is known as The Empire Builder.
KnownFor: It is known for its route between Chicago and Seattle or Portland.
It is known for its historical significance as the flagship passenger train of the Great Northern Railway.
It is known for carrying 338,993 passengers in fiscal year 2023.
It is known for its service to the Pacific Northwest.
It is known for its route through Glacier National Park.

UsedAs: It is used as a passenger train.
UsedFor: It is used for passenger rail service.
It is used for transporting passengers between Chicago and Seattle or Portland.
It is used for providing views of the Cascade Mountains and Glacier National Park.
It is used for volunteer tour guides to give commentary on points of interest.
UsedIn: It is used in Chicago.
It is used in Seattle.
It is used in Portland.
It is used in Spokane.

It is used in Fargo, North Dakota.
It is used in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.
It is used in St. Paul, Minnesota.

It is used in the Cascade Mountains.
It is used in Glacier National Park.

UsedTo: It is used to cut the scheduled 2,211-mile trip between Chicago and Seattle from 58.5 hours to 45 hours.
It is used to offer passengers sweeping views of the route.
It is used to improve its farebox recovery ratio.
It is used to run through Milwaukee via the Milwaukee Road.
It is used to operate west from the Twin Cities before turning northwest in Willmar.
It is used to reach Fargo.
It is used to reroute the Builder over the route between Fargo and Minot via Grand Forks.
It is used to complement the Builder and North Coast Hiawatha on the segment between Spokane and Seattle.
It is used to provide a three-times-daily service on a long-distance route outside the Silver corridor.
It is used to run combined with the Builder three days a week.
It is used to continue to serve St. Cloud, Staples and Detroit Lakes.
It is used to split in Spokane.
It is used to restore service to the line previously operated by the Spokane, Portland and Seattle Railway.
It is used to split in Spokane for much of the 1940s and 1950s.
It is used to include a wine and cheese tasting in the dining car for sleeping car passengers.
It is used to provide free newspapers in the morning.
It is used to give commentary on points of visual and historic interest.
It is used to return to its pre-pandemic daily schedule.
